skill.name: prd-doc-writer
skill.description: Write and iteratively refine PRD and requirements documents with story-driven structure and staged confirmations using ASCII wireframes and Mermaid diagrams
skill.summary: AI-powered product requirements document writer that guides users through structured discovery sessions with visual alignment
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: All 69 static analyzer findings are false positives. The detected patterns (backticks, markdown code fences, Mermaid diagram syntax) are documentation formatting, not executable code. This skill contains only Markdown reference templates and prompt instructions for AI-assisted PRD writing. No actual shell commands, network access, file operations, or cryptographic functions exist.

content.user_title: Create product requirements documents with AI guidance
content.value_statement: Product teams struggle to write clear, actionable requirements that developers can execute. This skill guides you through structured discovery sessions with visual alignment to produce complete PRDs.
actual_capabilities: Guide users through structured three-step confirmation workflow for PRD creation
Generate user journey maps with staged product milestones
Create Mermaid flowcharts, state diagrams, and sequence diagrams for requirement alignment
Draw ASCII wireframes for UI-related user stories
Write Gherkin-style acceptance criteria using Given-When-Then format
Maintain PRD registry with version tracking for project documentation
limitations: Cannot execute code or run shell commands - outputs are documentation only
Requires active user participation and domain knowledge to provide requirements
Does not integrate with external tools like Jira, Confluence, or design software
Cannot validate technical feasibility or estimate implementation effort

use_cases.title: Product Manager writing feature specifications
use_cases.description: A PM uses this skill to transform vague feature requests into structured PRDs with user stories, business rules, and acceptance criteria that engineering teams can execute without ambiguity.

use_cases.title: Startup founder documenting MVP requirements
use_cases.description: A non-technical founder leverages the guided discovery process to articulate their product vision, define user flows, and produce developer-ready documentation for outsourcing or hiring.

use_cases.title: Business Analyst gathering stakeholder requirements
use_cases.description: A BA uses the structured interview approach to extract detailed requirements from stakeholders, visualize processes with Mermaid diagrams, and create traceable documentation for compliance.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Start a new PRD from scratch
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to create a PRD for [feature name]. Help me define the user journey map and break it into development phases.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a greenfield requirements document with no existing structure

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Expand a user story with visual alignment
prompt_templates.prompt: For user story US-[number] about [feature], help me define the business rules and draw an ASCII wireframe for the UI layout.
prompt_templates.scenario: Fleshing out individual user stories with visual and behavioral specifications

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Create Mermaid state diagram for complex workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: My [object type] has multiple states including [list states]. Create a state diagram showing all valid transitions and help me define the rules for each transition.
prompt_templates.scenario: Modeling complex stateful systems like orders, approvals, or workflows

prompt_templates.title: Expert: Complete PRD generation with registry update
prompt_templates.prompt: We have completed all user story discussions. Generate the final PRD document and provide the registry row for docs/PRD_REGISTRY.md.
prompt_templates.scenario: Finalizing a complete PRD and updating the project documentation index

output_examples.input: User wants to define a login feature
output_examples.output: US-01: As a registered user, I want to log in with my email and password so that I can access my personalized dashboard. Business Rules: 1) Email must be verified before first login, 2) Password requires 8+ characters with mixed case, 3) Account locks after 5 failed attempts. Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN unverified user WHEN attempting login THEN shown verification reminder. ASCII wireframe shows email/password fields with login button and forgot password link.

output_examples.input: User needs a state diagram for order processing
output_examples.output: State Diagram: Draft -> Submitted -> Processing -> Shipped -> Delivered. Transitions include: Submit action moves Draft to Submitted, Payment confirmation moves Submitted to Processing, Warehouse fulfillment moves Processing to Shipped, Customer signature moves Shipped to Delivered. Exception paths: Payment failure returns Submitted to Draft, Return request moves Delivered to Processing.

best_practices: Always complete the three-step confirmation flow before generating final PRD to ensure alignment
Include both happy path and error handling scenarios in every user story acceptance criteria
Use ASCII wireframes for any UI-related story to prevent visual misalignment before development
anti_patterns: Generating full PRD documents before completing user story discussions and confirmations
Writing technical implementation details like API endpoints or database schemas in requirements
Skipping the user journey map and jumping directly to feature specifications without context

faq.question: What is the three-step confirmation workflow?
faq.answer: Step 1 defines the user journey map and gets phase alignment. Step 2 details each user story individually with single-point confirmation. Step 3 generates the final PRD only after explicit user approval.

faq.question: When should I use ASCII wireframes versus Mermaid diagrams?
faq.answer: Use ASCII wireframes for static UI layouts showing element positions and hierarchy. Use Mermaid diagrams for dynamic behaviors like user flows, state transitions, and time-sequenced interactions.

faq.question: Can this skill integrate with my existing project management tools?
faq.answer: No, this skill produces documentation only. It does not integrate with Jira, Asana, Confluence, or other tools. Output is Markdown that you can manually copy to your preferred platform.

faq.question: What is a PRD registry and do I need one?
faq.answer: A PRD registry is a single Markdown file that lists all PRDs in your project with version numbers and file links. It provides a central index for tracking which PRDs exist and where to find them.

faq.question: How detailed should user stories be?
faq.answer: Each user story must be self-contained with business rules, UI behavior if applicable, error handling paths, and Gherkin-style acceptance criteria covering success and failure scenarios.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for technical specifications or architecture docs?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on product requirements from a user perspective. For technical specs, consider using a different skill designed for system design and architecture documentation.
